items.q: What is characteristic of Baroque music?
items.correct: Continuous bass line, ornamentation, terraced dynamics, and counterpoint.
items.why: Students project later styles backwards. Baroque (1600-1750) used basso continuo, harpsichord/organ, and terraced (sudden) dynamics. Piano hadn't yet replaced harpsichord.

items.q: What happens in the recapitulation of sonata form?
items.correct: Both subjects return, but the second subject is now in the tonic key.
items.why: Students miss the KEY RESOLUTION. The exposition put subject 2 in a contrasting key; the recapitulation brings it back to the tonic, resolving the tonal tension - this is the dramatic point of sonata form.

items.q: How does a harmonic minor scale differ from a natural minor?
items.correct: The seventh degree is raised by a semitone (creating a leading note).
items.misconception: All notes are raised by a semitone.
items.why: Students think the whole scale changes. Only the 7TH is altered to create a leading note, which strengthens cadences in minor keys - producing the characteristic augmented second between 6th and 7th.

items.q: Which Miles Davis album popularised modal jazz?
items.why: Correct answer: Kind of Blue. “Bitches Brew” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which saxophonist is most associated with the development of bebop?
items.why: It's Charlie Parker. “John Coltrane” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year was Miles Davis's 'Kind of Blue' released?
items.why: The real figure is 1959. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
